summary: Iteration 15: Document upload bug fix verification. All features PASSED. Backend APIs (upload-id, upload-license) work correctly. Frontend profile page loads correctly with document upload section. File chooser opens when clicking upload buttons. Files are successfully uploaded and persisted in database.

issues: data-testid attributes on TouchableOpacity buttons (upload-id-btn, upload-license-btn) are not rendered in the DOM on web platform - React Native Web doesn't pass data-testid to underlying elements

backend_upload_id_api.details: Accepts file upload via multipart/form-data, returns id_photo as base64 data URI, persists to database

backend_upload_license_api.details: Accepts file upload via multipart/form-data, returns license_photo as base64 data URI, persists to database

frontend_profile_page_loads.details: Profile page at /tabs/profile loads correctly, shows 'Documents Obligatoires' section with ID card and license upload areas

frontend_upload_button_triggers_file_inp.details: Clicking 'Télécharger' or 'Modifier' buttons correctly triggers the hidden file input, opening the browser's file chooser dialog

frontend_file_selection_triggers_upload.details: After selecting a file, the upload completes successfully and UI updates to show uploaded image with 'OK' badge

frontend_hidden_file_inputs_present.details: Two hidden HTML <input type='file' accept='image/*'> elements are present in DOM for web platform file uploads

frontend_warning_message_display.details: Warning message 'Veuillez télécharger votre pièce d'identité et votre permis de conduire' correctly displayed when documents are missing

critical_code_review_comments: Minor: data-testid attributes on TouchableOpacity components are not rendered in the DOM on React Native Web. This is a known limitation - React Native Web doesn't automatically pass data-testid props to underlying DOM elements. Consider using 'testID' prop for native and adding 'accessibilityLabel' for web testing if needed in future.
seed_data_creation: Created new test user newuser_test_1772105887@test.com for testing fresh document upload flow
context_for_next_testing_agent: Document upload bug has been FIXED. The fix involved: 1) Passing File object directly from HTML input to store upload function instead of converting to blob URL, 2) Removing manual Content-Type header from axios calls so browser auto-sets multipart/form-data boundary, 3) Removing capture='environment' attribute from hidden file inputs. Both ID card and license uploads work correctly on web platform. Backend stores images as base64 data URIs in MongoDB. Profile page correctly shows 'OK' badges after upload and 'Télécharger'/'Modifier' buttons based on upload status.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The original implementation had three issues: 1) Converting File to blob URL before upload instead of passing File directly to FormData, 2) Manually setting Content-Type: multipart/form-data header which interfered with browser's auto-generated boundary, 3) The capture='environment' attribute on file inputs which is intended for mobile camera capture
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: 1) Modified handleWebFileChange to pass the File object directly to uploadIdCard/uploadLicense functions, 2) Removed manual Content-Type header from axios calls in authStore.ts, 3) Removed capture='environment' attribute from hidden file inputs, 4) Added instanceof File check in authStore.ts to handle File objects directly without fetch/blob conversion
rca_of_the_issue.verification: Verified via curl that backend APIs accept file uploads. Verified via Playwright that clicking upload buttons opens file chooser and files are successfully uploaded and persisted.
